name: gb200_nvl576_rail_inc
_comment: GB200 NVL576 rail-optimized 2-tier scale-up: 8 NVL72 pods on rail-aligned IB leaves (Quantum SHARP). Rail-optimized layout means each GPU's 8 NICs land on 8 distinct rail-leaves, and pods sharing the same rail-leaves see only single-leaf hops on cross-pod traffic — α_outer = α_leaf ≈ 2 μs (vs ~8 μs full spine traversal). For our 8-pod SuperPOD with rail-radix ≥ 8, all 8 pods share rail-leaves and the spine is never crossed for inter-pod collectives. Tier 0 = NVLink5 NVSwitch (72-port, 900 GB/s, 0.5 μs, NVLS sharp_class with 0.2 μs cut-through). Tier 1 = rail-leaf IB (8 ports, 400 GB/s per pod, 2.0 μs, Quantum SHARP with 0.4 μs cut-through). For collective groups ≤72 ranks the entire collective stays in tier 0; for groups 73–576 the dispatcher's hierarchical RS → sub-AR → AG composition uses the rail-leaf as the outer tier, with INC capability on both tiers when tuner.inc_enabled. See documentation/explaining/collectives/03_hierarchical_topologies.md Appendix A for the worked rail-optimized SuperPOD topology.
